rationale.root_cause: Although it identifies the memcpy at program.c:42 and the misuse of sizeof(*structCharVoid), it incorrectly claims corruption of the charFirst pointer and heap metadata instead of explaining that the overflow overwrites the subsequent struct fields (e.g., voidSecond). This misidentification of the affected field violates the requirement for a concrete mechanism in this file.
rationale.local_fix: It explicitly replaces the memcpy size with sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst), which limits the copy to the destination field’s size. This directly prevents overwriting the adjacent struct fields.
rationale.global_fix: It proposes using bounded copy helpers or a typed wrapper that tracks buffer length (e.g., size field plus memcpy_s/strlcpy), preventing confusion between struct and field sizes. This architectural change satisfies the global criterion.
